Question: Convert $$( -8 , -8 )$$ into polar coordinates and $$( 4 , \frac{2 \pi}{3} )$$ into rectangular coordinates.
Plot $$( 4 , \frac{5 \pi}{3} )$$ and $$( -3 , - \frac{7 \pi}{2} )$$ on the polar plane.
Create a graph of the curve defined by the function $$r = 4 + 4 \text{cos} \theta .$$
Answer: The name of this shape is a cardioid, which we will study further later in this section.

Question: Rewrite the equation $$r = \text{sec} \theta \text{tan} \theta$$ in rectangular coordinates and identify its graph.
Answer: $$y = x^{2} ,$$ which is the equation of a parabola opening upward.

Question: Determine the symmetry of the graph determined by the equation $$r = 2 \text{cos} ( 3 \theta )$$ and create a graph.
Answer: Symmetric with respect to the polar axis.
